docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: Professor is a backend service responsible for institution data management and configuration for Academia platform's Magnet scraping engine.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The service provides the necessary metadata and instruction sets for Magnet to navigate institutional portals and collect student information.

docs.title: Institution
docs.text:
An institution in the Professor context is a college or university supported by the Academia platform to provide students with relevant information.

  • Scoping: All data is scoped to an institution. No Magnet command or student profile can exist without a parent institution record.
  • Sourcing: Institutions are primarily provided by Verisafe via a RabbitMQ event queue.

docs.title: Magnet
docs.text:
Magnet is a scraping engine designed to simulate human interaction on web-based applications and extract relevant information upon request.

  • Programmable Browser: Built on top of a programmable browser that runs in either headless or interactive mode.
  • Instruction Execution: It executes specific commands provided by Professor to navigate complex UI flows and extract valuable data points.

docs.title: System WorkflowSync
docs.text:
1. Sync: Professor listens to RabbitMQ for institution updates from Verisafe.
  2. Configure: Our internal team define MagnetScrappingCommand objects containing JSON instructions.
  3. Execute: Magnet queries Professor for these instructions and executes them against the target portal.

docs.title: Exam timetable guidelines
docs.text: API for providing you with your school information.

docs.title: Introduction & Purpose
docs.text:
The Exam Timetable API provides an interface to ingest exam schedule data and serve it directly to the Academia app.

Who is this for? This endpoint is intended for institutional administrators and/or developers who need to enable exam tracking for their students. By following the data contract below, you can ensure your institution's schedules are seamlessly integrated into the Academia mobile.

docs.title: Data Contract
docs.text:
When submitting exam schedule data via the /api/exams/ingest/ endpoint, you must provide only the following fields:

{
  "items": [
    {
      "institution_id": "string (required, max 100 chars, non-empty)",
      "semester": "string (optional, e.g., 'Jan26') - Used to group exams and overwrite draft timetables.",
      "course_code": "string (required, non-empty)",
      "start_time": "ISO 8601 datetime (required)",
      "end_time": "ISO 8601 datetime (required)",
      "venue": "string (required, non-empty)",
      "coordinator": "string (optional)",
      "hrs": "string (required)",
      "raw_data": "object (optional, for institution-specific data)"
    }
  ]
}

The semester field accepts variations (e.g., JAN26, JAN-26, JAN/26); when uploading a final exam schedule, reuse the identical semester value so the system recognizes it as an update.

docs.title: Endpoint
docs.text:
POST /api/exams/ingest/

Submit a batch of exam schedules for ingestion.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
This endpoint requires an API key. Include it as the X-API-Key header on every request:

X-API-Key: your-api-key-here

Contact the administrator to obtain a valid API key. Requests without a valid key will be rejected.

Response:

{
  "message": "Ingestion completed successfully",
  "created_count": 150,
  "updated_count": 25,
  "skipped_count": 0
}

Status Codes:

Code Meaning 201 Created New records were created 200 OK Only updates occurred 400 Bad Request Invalid request payload 403 Forbidden Missing or invalid API key 500 Internal Server Error Server error during processing

docs.title: Incident Report: Exam Schedule Ingestion Performance Degradation
Severity
docs.text: High - Production API endpoint experiencing timeout failures

docs.title: Impact
docs.text:
• Affected Endpoint: POST /api/exams/ingest
  • Affected Users: Users were not directly affected however it was impossible to upload data in one go
  • Failure Rate: 100% for payloads containing ~1000 items (in this case Daystar university timetable data)
  • Response Time: >10 seconds, leading to database worker timeout
  • System Impact: Exam schedule ingestion requests failed completely

docs.title: Problem Statement
docs.text: When sending a JSON payload containing approximately 1,000 exam schedule items to the ingestion endpoint, the request would consistently exceed 10 seconds in execution time, ultimately resulting in a database worker timeout and complete request failure.

docs.title: 1. Sequential Processing (N+1 Query Pattern)
docs.text:
• Issue: The implementation used update_or_create() within a loop, resulting in two database round-trips per item (one SELECT query and one INSERT/UPDATE query)
  • Impact: For 1,000 items, this meant 2,000+ database queries

docs.title: 2. Long-Running Transactions
docs.text:
• Issue: The entire ingestion process was wrapped in a single transaction.atomic() block
  • Impact: This caused:
  • Lock contention on the ExamSchedule table
  • Increased memory overhead
  • Risk of transaction timeout for large datasets

docs.title: 3. Missing Database Indexes
docs.text:
• Issue: Lookups on (institution_id, semester, course_code) were not backed by a composite index
  • Impact: As the table grew, query execution degraded significantly

docs.title: 1. Bulk Operations
docs.text:
Refactored IngestExamScheduleView to leverage Django ORM's bulk_create() and bulk_update() methods:

  • Pre-fetch Strategy: Existing records are now fetched in a single query instead of per-item
  • Batch Create: New records are inserted in batches (batch_size=100)
  • Batch Update: Updated records are processed in batches with an explicit field list

Code Implementation: ```python

docs.title: Before: O(n) queries
docs.text: for item in items: obj, created = ExamSchedule.objects.update_or_create(...)

docs.title: After: O(1) + O(n/100) queries
docs.text: existing_exams = ExamSchedule.objects.filter( institution_id=institution_id, semester_id=semester_id, course_code__in=course_codes, # Single query ) ExamSchedule.objects.bulk_create(items_to_create, batch_size=100) ExamSchedule.objects.bulk_update(items_to_update, fields_to_update, batch_size=100)
